title: helianthus debilis subsp. cucumerifolius
intro: Helianthus debilis subsp. cucumerifolius, also known as the prairie sunflower, is a perennial plant native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and lance-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of Helianthus debilis subsp. cucumerifolius include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
